assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and can receive email.
The developer has not yet created a Red Hat account or provisioned a Developer Sandbox.
No OpenShift project, Deployment, or Route exists yet.

developer_goal: Create a Red Hat account, enter the free Developer Sandbox, and complete the official Get started with your Developer Sandbox learning path through source and container-image deployments.

first_success.outcome: The developer has entered the Developer Sandbox, opened a source-built application from Topology, and created a second Deployment from the specified container image.
first_success.observable_signal: The source-built application opens from the Topology external link, and the container-image Deployment is created in Topology.

route_selection.surface: Red Hat Developer Sandbox self-service web flow plus recommended learning path
route_selection.rule: Prefer the vendor-presented free Developer Sandbox over local or production OpenShift installs; use the recommended Get started learning path after Sandbox access.
route_selection.selected: Developer Sandbox access plus Get started with your Developer Sandbox learning path

candidates.name: Developer Sandbox + Get started learning path
candidates.reason: OpenShift getting-started and Sandbox surfaces point new developers to the free Sandbox and recommend this learning path.

candidates.name: OpenShift Local
candidates.reason: Requires local cluster installation rather than the no-setup Sandbox entry.

candidates.name: OpenShift Container Platform install
candidates.reason: Operator and infrastructure installation, not the free developer try path.

required_path.action: From Start your sandbox for free, register or sign in with a Red Hat account.

required_fields.label: Red Hat account registration or sign-in fields
required_fields.notes: Official access article says to complete registration and return but does not enumerate current fields.

required_path.observable_result: The developer is authenticated and can continue Sandbox provisioning.

required_path.action: Complete phone-number validation required for Developer Sandbox access.

required_fields.label: Phone number validation
required_fields.notes: FAQ requires a valid phone number; the verification channel and UI controls are not fully enumerated.

required_path.observable_result: Sandbox access is no longer blocked by phone validation.

required_path.action: Select Start using your sandbox, choose DevSandbox on the OpenShift Dedicated login page, then Launch the Red Hat OpenShift tile.
required_path.observable_result: The Developer Sandbox OpenShift console opens in the developer perspective.

required_path.action: In Topology, open Add to Project, choose Import from Git, enter an official sample repository URL, set Resource type to Deployment, and Create.
required_path.observable_result: OpenShift creates the source-based Deployment and begins building.

required_path.action: After the application icon ring turns dark blue, open the Topology external link for the source-built application.
required_path.observable_result: The browser displays the source-built application.

required_path.action: In Topology, open Add to Project, choose Container image, enter quay.io/rhdevelopers/intro-front, set Resource type to Deployment, and Create.

prerequisites.description: A free Developer Sandbox OpenShift cluster entitlement after Red Hat account and phone validation.
Access to the official sample Git repositories and quay.io/rhdevelopers/intro-front image named by the learning path.

external_gates.description: Red Hat account registration must succeed before Sandbox login.
Phone validation is required to reduce fraudulent Sandbox accounts.

unavoidable_waits.description: Sandbox provisioning and OpenShift builds must finish before Topology shows a ready application.

platform_outcomes.description: Developer Sandbox provisions an OpenShift environment after Start using your sandbox.
Import from Git and Container image flows create Deployments and Routes used by the learning path.

excluded.item: Reading OpenShift documentation and learning-path overview pages
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is evidence, not a counted product action.

excluded.item: OpenShift Local or production cluster installation
excluded.reason: The vendor try path is the no-setup Developer Sandbox.

excluded.item: Optional Sandbox experimentation after the terminal container-image resource
excluded.reason: First success ends at the learning-path terminal resource.

sources.title: Getting started with Red Hat OpenShift
Get started with your Developer Sandbox
How to access the Developer Sandbox for Red Hat OpenShift
Build your application from source code
Build your application from a container image

uncertainties.question: Which Red Hat account-registration fields and phone-verification UI appear for a fresh developer today?
uncertainties.impact: Account and verification action and field counts before Sandbox access cannot be verified.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Dated screenshots or a read-only walkthrough of current Red Hat registration and Sandbox phone validation from a fresh account.

uncertainties.question: Does first success require both learning-path resources, or is opening the source-built app alone the earliest meaningful outcome?
uncertainties.impact: Including the container-image resource adds Topology actions after an already observable deployed application.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Human judgment against SELECTION-POLICY first-success rules for whether the vendor learning-path terminal overrides the earlier Topology external-link success.
